highlights.text: I wrote on Friday: “Bear case Monday: Shorter-term, ES can dip on the failure of 6453…For the 6453 short ideally one needs to see either a test there and strong bounce first.
Most the strength last week was driven by my core setup (the Failed Breakdown) which occurred after Nvidia earning last Wednesday.
I wrote at 3:20pm last Wednesday prior to NVDA earnings: “The Failed Breakdown is obvious: It is of today’s 6474 low.” What did we do last Wednesday?
We flushed 6474 after Nvda, recovered in a Failed Breakdown and squeezed.
As readers know though, every “elevator down” sell in ES *always* sees an equally powerful squeeze and after the overnight flush we got it at 9:50AM.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the powerful Failed Breakdown we had after Nvda earnings (this was absolutely textbook and we will see this sequence many, many more times this year).

post_title: Fall Trading Starts With An SPX Flush and It Was Bought. Dead Cat Bounce, Or Bottom? September 3rd Plan
